mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains persistently above 310 while the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the real policy rate turns restrictive, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, with mean return greater than 0.05%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the predictive slope coefficient turns negative over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand strength, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.5% compared to a baseline model, or directional accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO falls by more than 0.5%.
